title: Rebuild GestureHotkeyApp from source after environment setup
bootstrap_instructions: Read ai-executor/AGENTS.md before starting work if available.
Read ai-executor/AI_ORCHESTRATOR_RUNTIME.md because this task is executed via AI Orchestrator runtime JSON if available.
Read the target repository docs in this order: README.md, docs/codex-handoff-spec.md, docs/progress-log.md, docs/test-result-summary.md, docs/openmousegesture-mod-plan.md.
Search the repository for any other handoff, TASK, task, report, progress, Codex, Claude, or instruction documents before changing structure.
If a referenced file cannot be read, do not infer its contents. Record the unreadable file in runtime/report.json.
purpose: Retry the Open Mouse Gesture / GestureHotkeyApp restart after build environment setup, rebuild the executable and NSIS installer from GitHub source, and continue minimal repository normalization for AI Orchestrator operation.
background: The old PC that produced prior build artifacts is unavailable. Treat old local exe/installer artifacts as unrecoverable and rebuild from the GitHub source. Previous task agent-20260707-open-mouse-gesture-resume-baseline-001 was blocked because Codex sandbox helper and build tools were not visible. The user has since confirmed: node v24.18.0, npm 11.16.0, cargo/rustc 1.96.1, makensis, and VsDevCmd.bat at C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\BuildTools\Common7\Tools\VsDevCmd.bat. Runner should be started from a PowerShell where Codex sandbox helper directories and C:\Program Files (x86)\NSIS are on PATH.
scope: In scope: verify environment, sync repository safely, inventory repository and handoff documents, create minimal missing project-level operation docs if safe, run a fresh build from source, regenerate GestureHotkeyApp.exe and NSIS installer, and report next development task. Out of scope: destructive deletion, large folder moves, history rewriting, broad redesign, unrelated refactoring, action label overlay re-enablement, release publication, installer execution, and edits to ai-common/ai-skills/ai-knowledge/ai-executor.
constraints: Do not perform destructive git operations such as reset --hard, clean -fd, force push, branch deletion, or unreviewed mass deletion.
Do not move or delete legacy/ or release/source folders during this task. If reorganization is needed, propose a plan first in runtime/report.json.
Do not run the installer unless a human explicitly permits it during this task.
Do not upload artifacts to GitHub Releases or any external service.
Do not commit generated target/ or installer outputs unless explicitly instructed later.
Do not re-enable action label overlay. It is intentionally disabled due to prior freezes and cursor/trajectory issues.
Do not make broad UI redesigns or unrelated refactors.
If local git synchronization is blocked, stop before source changes and report the exact local state and safe recovery options.
Keep output concise. Record details in runtime/report.json rather than long chat-style narration.
required_changes: Verify codex-windows-sandbox-setup.exe, node, npm, cargo, rustc, makensis, and VsDevCmd.bat are visible in the execution environment.
Inspect local git state and synchronize with the repository default branch only if this can be done safely without destructive operations.
Search for handoff/instruction documents. Confirm docs/codex-handoff-spec.md and list any additional candidates found.
Inventory the top-level repository structure and classify directories/files as current source, release/reference, docs, artifacts, legacy, runtime, or unknown.

If PROJECT.md is missing, create a concise PROJECT.md that states the current project purpose, active work directory, current source of truth, implemented state, intentionally disabled features, and next priorities.
If CHANGELOG.md is missing, create a concise CHANGELOG.md seeded from docs/progress-log.md at a summary level. Do not duplicate the full progress log.

If AGENTS.md is missing, create a minimal project-level AGENTS.md for execution agents that points to ai-executor rules, identifies the active work directory, and warns not to mix legacy WPF work into the current OpenMouseGesture line.
If docs/runtime-json-contract.md is missing, create a small project-local note pointing to ai-orchestrator runtime JSON roles. Do not invent a conflicting schema.

From source-v1.0.1/7-rate-OpenMouseGesture-b8f5357, run npm install if needed, npm run build, then npm run tauri build or artifacts/run-tauri-build.cmd when the Visual Studio Build Tools environment is required.
Regenerate GestureHotkeyApp.exe and the NSIS installer from source. Do not rely on any pre-existing target/release outputs.
Verify generated artifact paths, file sizes, and timestamps.
If the build fails due to a small source/config issue inside the declared target area, apply a minimal fix and rerun the relevant build command.
validation: Record commands run and their pass/fail result.
Verify npm run tauri build or run-tauri-build.cmd result.
Check for source-v1.0.1/7-rate-OpenMouseGesture-b8f5357/src-tauri/target/release/GestureHotkeyApp.exe after the fresh build.
Check for source-v1.0.1/7-rate-OpenMouseGesture-b8f5357/src-tauri/target/release/bundle/nsis/GestureHotkeyApp_0.1.0_x64-setup.exe after the fresh build.

If source-v1.0.1/7-rate-OpenMouseGesture-b8f5357/src-tauri/target/release/bundle/nsis/GestureHotkeyApp-setup.exe exists after the fresh build, report it; if not, explicitly report that it does not exist.
Separate verified results from unverified items.
completion_criteria: runtime/report.json is updated with success, failed, or blocked status for this task_id.
Git synchronization status is clearly reported.
Fresh build from GitHub source has either generated exe/installer or the blocker is clearly identified.
Any changed files are listed explicitly.
Unverified items and next recommended task are listed explicitly.
No destructive git operation, release upload, installer execution, or overlay re-enablement was performed.
reporting_requirements: Write a concise runtime/report.json including: task_id, status, summary, environment visibility, primary handoff document, repository structure inventory, normalization changes, git_sync result, commands run, files changed, validation results, regenerated artifact paths with file sizes/timestamps if available, unverified items, blockers, and recommended next task. Also update runtime/progress.json as appropriate during execution.
notes: Start from GitHub source and rebuild. Use docs/codex-handoff-spec.md as the known primary handoff candidate, but still search for other handoff/instruction files. Do not delete clutter during this task. Prefer adding current-operation entrypoint files and reporting a safe cleanup plan.
